# PrimaSTEM - Full Site Content

> Screen-free coding and mathematics for children ages 4 to 10. Wooden programmable robot with tangible NFC command tokens. Made in Cannes, France.

PrimaSTEM is a screen-free coding robot. Children program it by hand, and the same coding goes further than other screen-free robots: not just moving the robot and a few set turns, but any distance, any angle, and real arithmetic, so the programming runs straight into real maths. One robot covers the whole of ages 4 to 10.

---

# Home (/)

Coding and math without a screen. Teaches coding, logic, arithmetic, and geometry by hand. Ages 4 to 10.

The only screen-free tool that teaches both coding and mathematics. Made from wood. Sold wholesale to distributors.

## Two levels, one device

From a maze at four to a spiral at ten. One purchase, the whole of primary school.

Logic, Numbers, Ages 4 to 7: logical step 15 cm (adjustable), 90 degree turns. Mazes, sequences, debugging.

Mathematics, Coding, Ages 7 to 10: precise distances and angles of any value. Arithmetic, geometry, marker drawing.

## The robot draws what the child programs

That spiral? No other screen-free robot can draw it, and a child programmed it by hand. Pentagon, Star, Spiral, Heart.

Example: (Repeat 5) [(Forward) (50), (Right) (144)] = a star.

## Works with the maps and lesson plans you already have

PrimaSTEM adapts to the content of other educational robots. Step size is adjustable: 10, 12, 15, 25 cm. Any grid map works. A ready-made curriculum for the youngest comes in the box, and lesson plans built for another robot work on PrimaSTEM too.

## See it in action

Watch how children learn coding and math with PrimaSTEM. Video recorded with a prototype. Final product may differ.

## Choose your path

Complete kit: robot, control board, command tokens, lesson plans and teacher's guide.

School: available through our distributors. Robot, control board, tokens. Lesson plans and teacher's guide online.

Distributor: sold wholesale. Start with a small first order, no big commitment; volume unlocks a better wholesale price and territory exclusivity. You set your own retail for your market.

Parent: available through our distributors. Complete kit, ready to use. No subscriptions, no apps, no hidden costs. Lasts through primary school.

## Trusted by our partners

Early pilots and partners across Europe. Partners: Ministry of Education Luxembourg, La Compagnie du Code, Terra Numerica, A4 Education, AFINEF.

We make what children really need. Tangible, beautiful, real. Made in Cannes, France.

---

# Product (/product)

The screen-free coding robot where the coding becomes real maths. Children program it by hand, and the same coding goes into any distance, any angle and real arithmetic, so programming turns into real math. One wooden robot, ages 4 to 10.

## How PrimaSTEM works

1. Place tokens left to right. Green LED = accepted. The board speaks the command.
2. Press "Start." The robot executes. LEDs show which command is running.
3. Check the result. Wrong? Find the error, swap a token, run again.

## What's in the kit

Ladybug robot: 125 mm diameter, wood, USB-C charging, approximately 2 hours battery, depending on usage.
Control board: 317 x 217 mm, wood, 11 dual slots, Bluetooth ~5 m.
Command tokens: 33 x 33 mm, cardboard, NFC 13.56 MHz, Scratch color coding, writable via phone app.
Teacher's guide: lesson plans online, activities and printables, docs.primastem.com, 10 languages.

## Commands

Movement and Program: Forward, Backward, Left, Right, Random, Function, Pause.
Repeat: 2 to 999, Random repeat 1 to 6.
Mathematics: +, -, x, /, root, power
Numbers: 1 to 999
System: default step size, calibration.

## Drawing

Slip in a marker and every move leaves a line. Forward 50, Right 144, Repeat 5 makes a star; add an arithmetic progression and the star becomes a spiral. The robot works out the angles and distances as it goes, drawing real geometry on paper. A plain rolling robot with no screen cannot match that precision, and the child sees the result of their program right there on the page.

## Works with any map

Step size is adjustable: 10, 12, 15, 25 cm, any grid. Use maps from any robot already in your classroom. There's a ready-made curriculum in the box for the little ones, and lesson plans built for another robot work on PrimaSTEM too.

## Open platform

Every token is a standard NFC tag (13.56 MHz). Write any code using a free phone app. Create your own numbers, loops or arithmetic operations. NFC tags on the playing field: the robot plays audio when it drives over them. Language cards, musical fields, story quests.

There is also Scratch for the robot at scratch.primastem.com, so older kids keep coding with blocks well past age ten.

## Voice, words and music

The board speaks each token as it's placed. It also reads words aloud (around ninety of them), names the letters of the alphabet, plays musical notes, and reads arithmetic out loud. Available in 17 languages: English, Francais, Russkij, Ukrainska, Deutsch, Espanol, Italiano, Portugues (Brasil), Nederlands, Norsk, Polski, Svenska, Turkce, Dansk, Catala, Japanese, Hebrew. Helps children who can't read yet and children with visual impairments. Braille version in pilot testing.

## Specifications

Material: Wood. Robot: 125 mm diameter, 44 mm H. Control board: 317 x 217 mm. Tokens: 33 x 33 mm, cardboard, NFC 13.56 MHz. Connectivity: Bluetooth ~5 m. Battery: Li-Ion USB-C, approximately 2 hours, depending on usage. Default step: 15 cm (any value, adjustable). Angles: 1 to 360 degrees. Mathematics: +, -, x, /, root, power, factorial. Slots: 6x2 main + 5x2 function. Audio: voice feedback in 17 languages + sound samples. Certification: CE + UKCA. Age: 4 to 10. Made in: Cannes, France.

Tools: web simulator (simulator.primastem.com), web control (control.primastem.com), Scratch (scratch.primastem.com), firmware update (update.primastem.com).

Complete kit, ready to use. Available through distributors.

---

# Distributors (/distributors)

The screen-free robot that teaches real math, not just arrows. One SKU sells into every primary buyer: schools, preschools, Montessori, after-school, parents. Coding + mathematics. Ages 4 to 10. Wood. CE + UKCA certified. 17 languages. Sold wholesale to distributors.

## The market

Screen-free STEM is moving from preference to policy. Governments are pulling screens out of classrooms, and PISA 2022 shows math scores sliding across Europe. The demand is already on your customers' desks. Plenty of robots can turn and move forward; almost none teach math. PrimaSTEM does: any angle, arithmetic, geometry, freehand drawing, from age 4 to 10 on a single product.

## What sells

Two age groups, one device. Works with existing maps. The robot draws (hand a buyer the marker, run one spiral, the room goes quiet). Teachers create their own tokens. Braille version in pilot. PrimaSTEM covers both ages and replaces several robots: one purchase for the whole of primary school, around six years. Drawing, geometry, voice and the teaching method are all in the base kit, with no paid add-on packs and no second budget for extras. It also reads words aloud, names letters and plays notes in more than seventeen languages.

## Curriculum included

A ready-made curriculum comes with every PrimaSTEM for the youngest kids, in the LOGO and Montessori tradition, viewable before you order. Lesson plans and activities built for any other educational robot run on PrimaSTEM as they are.

## Mistakes become the lesson

The program sits on the board, in front of the child. When the robot goes the wrong way, the child finds the step that's off, swaps a token, and runs it again. With a marker in, the mistake shows up on paper at once. Kids learn to debug instead of fearing mistakes.

## Why PrimaSTEM

PrimaSTEM is a coding robot children program by hand, screen-free from the first command. The difference is how far the same coding goes: not just moving the robot and turning it a few set ways, but any distance, any angle, and real arithmetic, so the programming runs straight into real maths. One robot carries that from age 4 to 10. (Comparison table on the page versus Cubetto and Matatalab.)

## Sales materials

Everything to put PrimaSTEM in front of a school and close it: demo it live in a browser (simulator.primastem.com), show a real class using it (YouTube), drop the marketing kit into your catalogue (github.com/primastem/primastem-marketing), point teachers to the lesson plans (docs.primastem.com), keep the spec sheet on hand (/sheet), answer the usual questions (FAQ).

## Your margin

Start small, grow into better terms. The first order is low-minimum and prepaid, so no cash sits in dead stock while you test your market. You price for that market, we don't second-guess it. As volume climbs into the hundreds, the wholesale price improves and territory exclusivity becomes available. Marketing materials in your language. Technical support. Joint participation at trade shows. Early pilots and partners: Luxembourg (Ministry of Education pilot), Belgium (Code N Play), France (Terra Numerica). All terms and pricing are discussed individually.

---

# Schools (/schools)

Coding and mathematics, ages 4 to 10. One device. A screen-free robot children program by hand, where the coding goes all the way into real maths. One purchase covers the whole of primary. Available through our distributors.

## What children do

They place command tokens on the board, that's their program, visible in full. Press "Start." The robot executes. Wrong result? Find the error, swap a token, run again. Sequences, functions, loops, debugging. No screen. Hands only. Because the whole program is in plain sight, children find the wrong step themselves, and with a marker in, the slip shows on paper at once. They learn to fix mistakes instead of fearing them.

## Two levels

Ages 4 to 7: Logic. Logical steps, 90 degree turns. Forward, backward, left, right, function, repeat. Planning, sequences, debugging, counting.
Ages 7 to 10: Mathematics. Precise distances in mm and any angle in degrees: 1 to 360. Arithmetic: +, -, x, /, root, power. Negative numbers. Progressions. Insert a marker, the robot draws polygons, stars, spirals, fractals. A plain rolling robot with no screen cannot match that precision. And after primary, the same robot keeps going: with Scratch (scratch.primastem.com), older students program it with blocks, past age ten.

## Lessons included

Every kit comes with lessons ready to teach, built for the youngest kids in the spirit of LOGO and Montessori. Open it and teach the same day, no late-night prep. Already use another robot? The lesson plans and maps you have work on PrimaSTEM as they are. Everything is online, in several languages, viewable before you order. Everything is in the base kit: no paid add-on packs, no second budget for extras. Full documentation at docs.primastem.com.

## What teachers don't expect

Open platform. Sound maps. Voice feedback in 17 languages (also reads words aloud, names letters, plays notes). Braille version in pilot testing.

Available through our distributors. Volume pricing on request. Rental available.

---

# Parents (/parents)

Real coding and math, by hand, no screen. A wooden robot, no app, that grows with your child from 4 to 10. Sold through distributors: tell us where you are and we will point you to one.

## What is it

Tokens on a wooden board: "forward," "turn right," "repeat 3 times." That's the program. The child sees it, touches it, rearranges it. Presses a button, the robot executes. Something went wrong? Find the mistake, fix it, run again. The program is right there in front of them, so a child learns to sort it out alone, calmly, without the fear of getting it wrong.

## Grows with your child

At 4 to 5: simple commands and numbers. Mazes on colorful maps.
At 6 to 7: functions and loops.
At 8 to 10: mathematics. Precise angles, distances, arithmetic. The robot draws stars, spirals, geometric patterns.
And it doesn't stop at ten: with Scratch (scratch.primastem.com), older kids keep programming the robot with blocks.

## You don't have to be a teacher

You don't need to know how to code. The box includes a simple guided course for little ones: short activities, one small step at a time, everything explained as you go. There are free printable activities online too.

## Why this one

Wood, not plastic. No screen, no apps. It speaks: reads words aloud, names letters, even plays notes, in your language. It draws stars, spirals, hearts, with a precision a plain rolling robot can't match. One purchase, everything in the box, no paid add-ons. Made in France, CE + UKCA certified.

Available through our distributors. Complete kit.

---

# About (/about)

French startup from Cannes building screen-free educational technology.

## We make tools that help children think

PrimaSTEM is inspired by Seymour Papert's constructionism and Montessori principles.

## How it started

We watched children spending more time in front of screens. We watched math scores fall. And we watched a child's eyes light up when they touched a wooden board and a robot, pressed a button, and the robot did exactly what they told it to.

## What we believe

Children learn by doing. Mathematics is beautiful. Technology should be open. Simplicity takes work.

## Where we are

Partners: Terra Numerica / INRIA / CNRS, Educazur, AFINEF. Early traction: Luxembourg (Ministry of Education pilot), Belgium (Code N Play), France. Trade shows: EDUCATECH (Paris), SIMO Educacion (Madrid), SpielwarenMesse (Nuremberg).

---

Email: info@primastem.com
WhatsApp: +33 6 24 95 09 36
Location: Cannes, France
YouTube: youtube.com/@primastem
Instagram: @primastem.robot

We respond within 24 hours. Working languages: English, Francais, Russkij.

# FAQ (/faq)

Q: What age is PrimaSTEM for?
A: From age 4. Ages 4 to 7 work with logic and simple programs. Ages 7 to 10 add mathematics, precise angles, and drawing.

Q: How does it work?
A: Children place command tokens on a wooden board, that's their program. Press Start, the robot executes. No screen, no apps.

Q: What's in the kit?
A: Ladybug robot (wood, USB-C, Bluetooth), control board with 11 dual slots, command tokens (cardboard, NFC, color-coded), and a teacher's guide with lesson plans.

Q: Does the robot draw?
A: Yes. Insert a marker and every movement leaves a line. Children program stars, spirals, polygons, fractals.

Q: Does it work with maps from other robots?
A: Yes. The default step size is adjustable: 10, 12, 15, 25 cm or any value.

Q: How much does it cost?
A: We sell wholesale to distributors and don't publish prices online. Contact us and we'll connect you with a distributor or send wholesale terms directly. Schools and families: available through our distributors.

Q: Can I rent instead of buying?
A: Rental is available for schools.

Q: Is there a distributor in my country?
A: We're just starting: early pilots and partners in France, Belgium and Luxembourg, with distributor conversations underway across Europe.

Q: How long does the battery last?
A: USB-C charging, approximately 2 hours per charge, depending on usage.

Q: Is it safe for children?
A: CE and UKCA certified. Made from wood. No screen. Recommended for ages 4+.

Q: Can teachers create their own tokens?
A: Yes. Every token is a standard NFC tag. Write any code using a free phone app.

Q: Can my child use it without a teacher?
A: Yes. Lessons available on docs.primastem.com in 10 languages. Video tutorials on YouTube.

---
